hero.headline: Dr. MGR Online MBA in Hospital and Healthcare Management: Fees, Syllabus and Career Scope
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme | NAAC A+ accredited | Rs 1,36,000 - Rs 1,41,000 total fee | Healthcare heritage institution since 2003

sections.tldr: Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute Online MBA in Hospital and Healthcare Management covers Healthcare Law and Ethics, Public Health System and Outreach in Sem 3, and Risk Management and Health Insurance in Sem 4. Common core includes Strategic Management, Entrepreneurship Development, Digital Marketing, International Business Management, and an 8-week mandatory Field Work and Project Work. Total fee Rs 1,36,000 to Rs 1,41,000 across 4 semesters. NAAC A+ accredited, UGC-DEB approved, Deemed University.

about.body:
Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute is a Deemed University in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, named after former Chief Minister Dr. M.G. Ramachandran. The institution was founded with a strong healthcare and medical education focus. Dr. MGR established multiple medical colleges and hospitals during his tenure, and this heritage carries into the university's academic DNA.

The Hospital and Healthcare Management specialisation is arguably the most natural fit within Dr. MGR's MBA portfolio. The curriculum covers Healthcare Law and Ethics, Public Health System and Outreach in Sem 3, and Risk Management and Health Insurance in Sem 4. Combined with the 8-week mandatory field project, healthcare professionals can apply learning directly in hospital or public health settings.

At Rs 1,36,000 to Rs 1,41,000 total, the programme sits in the mid-range for NAAC A+ online MBAs. The healthcare heritage gives Dr. MGR a credibility edge over universities offering healthcare management purely as a curriculum addition. The university's proximity to major Chennai hospitals (including its own affiliated medical institutions) provides contextual depth.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA core. Semesters 3-4 below show the Hospital and Healthcare Management specialisation subjects. Spec-specific subjects are listed first, followed by common core subjects.

subjects.name: Healthcare Law and Ethics
subjects.desc: Medical negligence, patient rights, clinical trial ethics, PCPNDT Act, MCI regulations, and healthcare compliance frameworks in India.

subjects.name: Public Health System and Outreach
subjects.desc: Primary healthcare infrastructure, National Health Mission, Ayushman Bharat, community health worker programmes, and public health outreach strategies.

subjects.name: Strategic Management
subjects.desc: Competitive strategy frameworks, industry analysis, corporate-level strategy, and strategic implementation in healthcare organizations.

subjects.name: Entrepreneurship Development
subjects.desc: Healthcare startup ideation, clinic and diagnostic centre business planning, funding strategies, and MSME support for health ventures.

subjects.name: Management Accounting
subjects.desc: Hospital cost accounting, budgetary control for healthcare facilities, variance analysis, and financial decision-making tools.

subjects.name: Production and Operations Management
subjects.desc: Hospital operations management, patient flow optimization, facility layout for healthcare, and capacity planning.

subjects.name: Skill Development
subjects.desc: Communication skills for healthcare settings, patient interaction techniques, and professional development modules.

subjects.name: Summer Project
subjects.desc: Industry-linked summer project in a healthcare organization requiring field research, data collection, and structured report.

subjects.name: Risk Management and Health Insurance
subjects.desc: Clinical risk assessment, hospital risk mitigation, health insurance models in India, TPA operations, Ayushman Bharat claims, and corporate health insurance design.

subjects.name: Digital Marketing
subjects.desc: Digital marketing for healthcare organizations, patient engagement strategies, healthcare SEO, and social media compliance.

subjects.name: International Business Management
subjects.desc: Global healthcare markets, medical tourism strategy, cross-border health services, and international healthcare regulations.

subjects.name: Contemporary Seminar
subjects.desc: Student-led seminar on current healthcare management topics. Research, presentation, and peer discussion.

subjects.name: Field Work and Project Work (8 Weeks)
subjects.desc: Mandatory 8-week field project in a hospital, clinic, or public health organization. Structured report, data analysis, and viva voce defence.

whoHires.body:
Dr. MGR MBA Hospital and Healthcare Management graduates target roles across hospital administration, public health, and health insurance:

**Industries:** Multi-specialty hospitals, primary healthcare chains, public health departments, health insurance (TPA and direct), pharmaceutical companies, medical device firms, diagnostic chains, and telemedicine platforms

**Entry-level roles:** Hospital Administrator (junior), Healthcare Executive, Health Insurance Officer, Public Health Coordinator, Medical Records Manager, Patient Relations Officer

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years):** Hospital Operations Manager, Health Insurance Manager, Public Health Programme Manager, Quality Manager (NABH), Healthcare Consultant, TPA Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** Hospital CEO/COO, Director of Operations (hospital chain), VP Healthcare Services, Chief Quality Officer, Head of Health Insurance

**Employers:** Apollo Hospitals, Fortis, MIOT Hospitals, Kauvery Hospital, Global Hospitals, Star Health Insurance, government health departments (Tamil Nadu and central), and Chennai/South India diagnostic chains.

**Note:** Dr. MGR's healthcare heritage and Chennai location provide direct access to one of India's largest hospital ecosystems.

skills.body:
The Hospital and Healthcare Management specialisation builds skills across hospital operations, public health, and health insurance:

**Technical skills:**
- Healthcare Law: medical negligence assessment, patient rights frameworks, clinical trial ethics review, MCI/NMC compliance, and PCPNDT Act application
- Public Health: National Health Mission programme design, Ayushman Bharat scheme operations, community health outreach planning, and epidemiological data interpretation
- Health Insurance: TPA claim processing, health insurance product design, Ayushman Bharat claims management, and corporate health policy structuring

**Applied skills:**
- Hospital Operations: patient flow management, OPD/IPD scheduling, hospital supply chain, NABH quality standards, and facility layout optimization
- Risk Management: clinical risk identification, incident reporting systems, hospital safety protocols, and infection control compliance

**Soft skills:** Patient communication, interdepartmental coordination in hospitals, presenting health outcomes data to non-clinical stakeholders, and managing healthcare teams across clinical and administrative functions.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Dr. MGR Online MBA Hospital and Healthcare Management against peer programmes:

**vs Vignan Online MBA Healthcare Management (Rs 90,000):** Vignan is Rs 46,000 to Rs 51,000 cheaper. Vignan covers Healthcare SCM, Medical Records, Healthcare IT, and Medical Tourism. Dr. MGR covers Healthcare Law/Ethics, Public Health, and Health Insurance. Dr. MGR's healthcare heritage (the university was founded with medical education focus) adds institutional credibility. Vignan wins on fee. Dr. MGR wins on healthcare pedigree.

**vs NIU Online MBA Healthcare Management (Rs 97,600):** NIU is Rs 38,400 to Rs 43,400 cheaper. Both are UGC-DEB approved. NIU is a private university in Greater Noida. Dr. MGR has NAAC A+ (higher accreditation) and healthcare institutional heritage. Dr. MGR wins on accreditation and healthcare credibility.

**vs Sharda Online MBA Healthcare Management (Rs 1,54,500):** Sharda is Rs 13,500 to Rs 18,500 more expensive. Both are UGC-DEB approved. Sharda has NCR location advantage. Dr. MGR has NAAC A+ and healthcare heritage. For South India healthcare careers, Dr. MGR is the stronger choice at a lower fee.

fees.body:
The Dr. MGR Online MBA in Hospital and Healthcare Management is priced between Rs 1,36,000 and Rs 1,41,000 total across 4 semesters (2-year programme). Fee range varies slightly by specialization - contact our counsellor for the exact figure for your chosen track.

**Payment breakdown**:
- **Per semester**: Rs 34,000 - Rs 40,000 across 4 semesters
- **Full upfront option**: Rs 1,36,000 - Rs 1,41,000 one-time

**Additional charges**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time, non-refundable at admission)

**Zero-cost EMI**: Available directly through Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute starting Rs 5,700 per month. No external marketing partner or NBFC intermediary.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Dr. MGR Online each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees for Hospital and Healthcare Management with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Hospital and Healthcare Management as the specialisation, issued by Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute (Deemed University) under UGC-DEB online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A+ accreditation, UGC-DEB approval, and Deemed University status since 2003. Valid for hospital administration roles, government health department positions, NABH quality officer requirements, and private healthcare management careers. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from Dr. MGR Online MBA Hospital and Healthcare Management students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Priya
items.liked: Healthcare Law and Ethics subject was directly applicable to my hospital compliance role. Dr. MGR's medical heritage in Chennai gives the degree extra credibility with hospital HR departments
items.disliked: Only three healthcare-specific subjects across Sem 3 and 4. Would have preferred more clinical management depth
items.body: I work at a multi-specialty hospital in Chennai. Dr. MGR's name carries weight in Tamil Nadu healthcare circles. The Healthcare Law and Ethics course covered medical negligence and patient rights at a practical level. Public Health System covered Ayushman Bharat operations, which is relevant for any hospital dealing with government insurance. The 8-week field project in my own hospital let me apply learnings immediately.

items.name: Rajesh
items.liked: Risk Management and Health Insurance in Sem 4 covered TPA operations and claims processing at the detail level I needed for my insurance role
items.disliked: Outside Tamil Nadu and South India, Dr. MGR is not well-known compared to Apollo-affiliated or Manipal programmes
items.body: The healthcare management curriculum is compact but covers the right areas: law, public health, insurance. The common core subjects (Strategic Management, Digital Marketing) add general management breadth. Fee at Rs 1.36-1.41L is reasonable for NAAC A+. The 8-week project is the highlight. For pan-India healthcare brands, you might want a nationally recognized university name. For TN-based healthcare careers, Dr. MGR works well.

items.name: Deepa
items.liked: Public Health System and Outreach covered National Health Mission and community health worker programmes at a level useful for my PHC administration work
items.disliked: LMS interface needs improvement. Some study materials could be more current with 2024-era healthcare regulations
items.body: At this fee, Vignan at Rs 90,000 and NIU at Rs 97,600 offer healthcare MBAs cheaper. Dr. MGR's advantage is the healthcare institutional heritage and Tamil Nadu brand. If you are a healthcare professional in TN, the brand recognition helps. The curriculum covers essentials. Delivery quality is adequate. For candidates outside South India, weigh the fee against national brand alternatives.

faqs.question: Is Dr. MGR a Deemed University?
faqs.answer: Yes. Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute has operated as a Deemed University under UGC recognition since 2003. Named after former Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Dr. M.G. Ramachandran.

faqs.question: What is the fee difference between Rs 1.36L and Rs 1.41L?
faqs.answer: The total fee varies slightly by specialization. Contact our counsellor to confirm the exact fee for Hospital and Healthcare Management before making any payment.

faqs.question: Is Dr. MGR Online MBA UGC-DEB approved?
faqs.answer: Yes. Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute is UGC-DEB approved for online programmes. The degree is legally equivalent to a campus MBA under UGC 2020 guidelines.

faqs.question: Why is Dr. MGR a good fit for healthcare management?
faqs.answer: Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute was founded with a strong healthcare and medical education focus. The university runs affiliated medical colleges and hospitals in Chennai. This healthcare heritage gives the Hospital and Healthcare Management MBA institutional credibility that purely general universities lack.

faqs.question: What does the 8-week Field Work and Project Work involve for healthcare students?
faqs.answer: Healthcare management students typically complete the 8-week field project at a hospital, clinic, diagnostic centre, or public health organization. The project requires structured data collection, analysis, a written report, and a viva voce defence.

chooseIf: You work in healthcare (hospital, clinic, public health, insurance) and want an MBA from a university with medical education heritage.
You are based in Tamil Nadu or South India where Dr. MGR's brand carries strong recognition in healthcare circles.
NAAC A+ accreditation at a mid-range fee (Rs 1.36-1.41L) meets your requirements.
The 8-week mandatory field project in a healthcare setting is valuable for your career development.
consider: Vignan at Rs 90,000 or NIU at Rs 97,600 offer healthcare MBAs at significantly lower fees.
Sharda at Rs 1,54,500 has NCR location advantage if you target North India healthcare employers.
Pan-India brand recognition outside Tamil Nadu is limited. For national healthcare chains, consider universities with stronger all-India presence.
Only three spec-specific healthcare subjects across Sem 3-4. If deeper clinical management coverage is needed, compare syllabi carefully.
